prompts: Groundwater circulation involves the movement of water through underground layers known as aquifers. When rainwater or surface water percolates through soil and rock, it reaches these aquifers, where it is stored. This process is called percolation. Natural recharge occurs when the water flowing through these aquifers replenishes them, maintaining the groundwater levels. Aquifers can be confined or unconfined; confined aquifers are trapped between impermeable layers, while unconfined aquifers have direct access to surface water. Groundwater can be extracted for human use but is also naturally discharged into springs, rivers, and lakes, sustaining ecosystems.
